creator: jdp@polstra.com
text:
Dirk Meyer wrote:

> Here again, and more detailed:

Thanks!

> I used the "modules" names instead of the cvs pathes.
> 
> affected modules:
> de-webalizer2   ports/german/webalizer2
> webalizer       ports/www/webalizer
> webalizer-2     ports/www/webalizer-2
> 
> cp $CVSROOT/ports/www/webalizer-2/files/patch-aa,v \
>       $CVSROOT/ports/www/webalizer/files/patch-aa,v
> cp $CVSROOT/ports/www/webalizer-2/files/patch-ab,v \
>       $CVSROOT/ports/www/webalizer/files/patch-ab,v
> cp $CVSROOT/ports/www/webalizer-2/files/patch-ac,v \
>       $CVSROOT/ports/www/webalizer/files/patch-ac,v
> cp $CVSROOT/ports/www/webalizer-2/files/patch-ad,v \
>       $CVSROOT/ports/www/webalizer/files/patch-ad,v

OK, I can do those 4 files.  But ...

> mv $CVSROOT/ports/german/webalizer2 $CVSROOT/ports/german/webalizer

I don't think it's possible to do that.  As far as I know, we can only
perform repository copies in cases where the destination file does not
already exist.  If we copied onto an existing file in the repository,
it would wipe out all of the history in the original file.

Do you still want me to copy "ports/www/webalizer-2/files/patch-a[a-d]",
even though the "ports/german/..." files cannot be copied?

John
